text:
To compute \( \binom{12}{2} \), we use the formula for combinations:
\[ \binom{n}{r} = \frac{n!}{r!(n-r)!} \]
For \( \binom{12}{2} \):
\[ \binom{12}{2} = \frac{12!}{2!(12-2)!} = \frac{12!}{2! \cdot 10!} = \frac{12 \times 11 \times 10!}{2 \times 1 \times 10!} \]
The \(10!\) terms cancel out:
\[ \binom{12}{2} = \frac{12 \times 11}{2 \times 1} = \frac{132}{2} = 66 \]

The corrected intermediate value is \(12 \times 11 = 132\), and the final answer is:
\[ \boxed{66} \]
